svg.text: A visual framework of algorithm types, domains and real-world applications
SUPERVISED LEARNING - learns from labeled examples
Fits a straight line through data points to predict a continuous numeric output. Minimizes the difference
between predicted and actual values using the least squares method.
House price prediction Sales forecasting Risk scoring Stock return estimation
Uses a sigmoid function to output probabilities for binary classification. Draws a decision boundary
separating two classes based on input features. Foundation for many neural network architectures.
Spam detection Disease diagnosis Loan default prediction Sentiment classification
Splits data using a tree of yes/no questions based on feature values. Each branch narrows possibilities
until a leaf node yields a prediction. Highly interpretable - every decision step is traceable.
Fraud detection Medical diagnosis Customer churn prediction Product classification
An ensemble of many decision trees trained on random data subsets. Each tree votes and the majority
result wins - reducing overfitting and boosting accuracy significantly over a single tree.
Credit scoring Stock prediction Disease risk assessment Image feature classification
Finds the optimal hyperplane that maximizes the margin between two classes. Uses kernel tricks
to handle non-linearly separable data by projecting into higher-dimensional feature space.
Text classification Handwriting recognition Bioinformatics Anomaly detection
UNSUPERVISED / DEEP LEARNING - finds patterns and learns representations
Partitions data into K groups by iteratively assigning points to the nearest centroid, then updating
centroids until stable. No labels required - discovers natural groupings in data.
Customer segmentation Document clustering Anomaly detection Market basket analysis
Uses convolutional filters to scan images and detect spatial features (edges, textures, shapes). Each
layer learns progressively higher-level features - from edges to textures to complete objects.
Image classification Object detection Facial recognition Medical imaging (X-ray, MRI)
Processes sequential data by maintaining a hidden state (memory) across time steps. LSTMs use
gating mechanisms to retain long-range dependencies without vanishing gradient problems.
Language translation Speech recognition Time-series forecasting Music generation
Uses self-attention to weigh relationships between all tokens simultaneously. Pre-trained on massive
text data then fine-tuned for tasks. Powers ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini and modern AI assistants.
Chatbots Text generation Summarization Code generation Question answering
Two networks compete: a Generator creates fake data, a Discriminator judges real vs fake. Through
adversarial training the generator improves until outputs are indistinguishable from real data.
Image synthesis Deepfakes Data augmentation Art generation Drug discovery
Algorithms 1-5: Supervised Learning | 6-7: Unsupervised and Computer Vision | 8-10: NLP and Generative AI
Created as a portfolio artifact - Machine Learning Foundations Course
